visits.text: Arrival in Banlung - 'the red town' due to the laterite soil that stains everything, capital of Ratanakiri nicknamed 'the Khmer Far West', end-of-the-world atmosphere

Yeak Laom volcanic lake - 700,000-year-old crater, circular lake 800 meters in diameter and 48 meters deep with crystal-clear waters, considered sacred by the Tampuen tribes; 2.5 km hiking trail around the lake, swimming allowed
Kachang Waterfall - 6 km from Banlung, waterfall in dense forest creating a veil of mist, peaceful pool at the base for swimming
Ka Tieng Waterfall - near Kachang, small cascade surrounded by large boulders, easy access by scooter on red dirt track
Banlung Market (minorities market) - every morning villagers from different ethnic groups (Tampuen, Braos, Kreung) come to sell fresh produce and traditional crafts
Ethnic villages of Ratanakiri - visit a Tampuen or Kreung village accessible by motorbike from Banlung, traditional houses, totemic cemeteries and timeless life in the forest

activities.text: Yeak Laom Lake Cultural Museum - small museum presenting the crafts of Ratanakiri tribes (musical instruments, baskets, hand-woven textiles), direct purchase from local artisans
Sunset from Wat Rahtanharahm - pagoda on the hill overlooking Banlung with reclining Buddha, panorama over the red lands and forest
Navigation on the Sesan River - motorboat excursion on the river bordered by tropical forest, stops in riverside villages inaccessible by road

days_plan.itin_title: Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Colonial center of Battambang - Cambodia's second-largest city, you'll wander streets lined with Franco-Khmer houses from the 1920s, the old railway station and Phsar Nath market
Phnom Sampeau - sacred mountain 12 km from town, you climb 358 steps to reach the hilltop pagodas with panoramic views over rice paddies; below, the Killing Caves recall the Khmer Rouge executions
Wat Ek Phnom - ruined 11th-century temple, sculpted lintels depicting the churning of the ocean of milk, located 11 km north of Battambang amid rice fields

activities.text: Bamboo Train (Norry) - bamboo platform propelled on old rails through rice paddies, roughly 7 km journey, a unique folkoric experience in Cambodia
Khmer cooking class - 3-hour workshop to learn how to prepare amok, lok lak and fresh spring rolls with ingredients from the local market
Bat spectacle at Phnom Sampeau - around 5:30 PM, millions of bats exit the cave in a continuous black ribbon for 30 to 45 minutes, a striking natural phenomenon

days_plan.itin_title: Essential Cambodia Circuit: Phnom Penh, Battambang and Angkor
Essential Cambodia Circuit: Phnom Penh, Battambang and Angkor
Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts

visits.text: Kampot town center - French colonial architecture from the 1920s along the Prek Kampot River, old French bridge, giant Durian roundabout, laid-back atmosphere
La Plantation - Kampot pepper farm (PGI since 2010), guided tour of the plots and tasting of fresh black, red, and white peppercorns on the vine

Bokor Mountain (Preah Monivong National Park) - hill station at 1,080 meters built by the French in 1922, you'll discover the abandoned palace, ruined church, and Lok Yeay Mao pagoda with views over the Gulf of Thailand
Phnom Chhnork Cave - 7th-century Hindu temple nestled in a natural cave, you descend 203 steps to discover a brick sanctuary dedicated to Shiva
Kep Crab Market - seaside market where fishermen sell live blue crab, you can savor it stir-fried with Kampot green peppercorns in adjacent restaurants
Kep National Park - 8 km hiking trail through tropical forest on the hill overlooking the sea, views of the Gulf of Thailand and Phú Quốc Island (Vietnam)

activities.text: Kayaking on Prek Kampot River (Green Cathedral) - 3 km loop through a natural mangrove canopy, calm water and filtered light
Sunset on Kampot River - settle into one of the floating bar-restaurants to watch the sun set behind the Bokor Mountains
Boat excursion to Koh Tonsay (Rabbit Island) - island 25 minutes from Kep, sandy beach lined with coconut palms, hammocks and grilled fish lunch with your feet in the sand

days_plan.itin_title: Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Koh Rong Samloem - more preserved island and less party-oriented than its neighbor Koh Rong, 45-minute ferry from Sihanoukville; Saracen Bay offers a crescent of white sand beach lined with coconut palms
Sunset Beach - isolated beach on the island's west coast, accessible only on foot via a 30-minute jungle trail or by boat, sunsets facing the open sea

activities.text: Snorkeling from the beach - coral reefs accessible by swimming around the island, tropical fish and sea turtles (with luck)
Night swimming with bioluminescent plankton - natural phenomenon visible on moonless nights, every movement in the water creates fluorescent blue sparkles
Trek through the island's inland jungle - trail crossing the tropical forest connecting Saracen Bay to Sunset Beach, endemic wildlife (geckos, butterflies, non-venomous snakes)
Sea kayaking along the coastline - exploring coves, cliffs and deserted beaches accessible only by sea

days_plan.itin_title: Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Stop in Skun ('Spiderville') - this town's market is famous for its fried spiders served as snacks; you can observe (or taste) the crispy tarantulas
Colonial town of Kratie - small town on the banks of the Mekong, French houses from the 1920s, peaceful riverside and lively morning market
Koh Trong Island - local ferry to cross the Mekong (5 minutes), stilt village, pomelo orchards and vegetable gardens, end-of-the-world atmosphere

activities.text: Boat excursion to Kampi to observe Irrawaddy dolphins - population of about 90 individuals in this stretch of the Mekong, best viewing at sunset when they surface to breathe
Bicycle loop around the island (≈ 9 km) - flat track along the Mekong, you'll pass water buffaloes, playing children, and rice paddies, with a possible stop at a local homestay

days_plan.itin_title: Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda - residence of the king, you'll discover the throne hall, gardens, and the pagoda's floor paved with 5,000 silver tiles (appropriate dress required)
National Museum of Cambodia - red sandstone building from 1920, world's largest collection of Angkorian sculptures, the 11th-century bronze Vishnu is the centerpiece
Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum (S-21) - former high school converted into a detention center by the Khmer Rouge (1975-1979), audio guide available in English, a harrowing but essential visit
Choeung Ek Memorial (Killing Fields) - execution site 15 km from the center, memorial stupa containing over 8,000 skulls, 45-minute audio tour
Wat Phnom - 27-meter artificial hill founded in 1373, pagoda surrounded by a shaded park at the heart of the city
SOSORO Money Museum - modern museum tracing Cambodia's economic history through its currencies, from barter to current banknotes, free admission
Russian Market (Tuol Tom Poung) - covered market favored by expats for textiles, antiques, and street food, last-minute souvenir shopping
Silk Island (Koh Dach) - island on the Mekong accessible by ferry from Chroy Changvar Bridge, traditional silk weaving workshops, weaving villages, and river beaches

activities.text: Sunset cruise on the Mekong - one-hour traditional boat ride at the confluence of the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers, departing from Sisowath Quay
Evening stroll along Sisowath Quay and street food at the night market - skewers, Cambodian crepes, and freshly pressed sugarcane juice
Central Market (Phsar Thmey) - Art Deco hall from the 1930s shaped like a yellow dome, jewelry, textiles, and street food
Architectural walk - walking circuit past the central post office, 1932 railway station, Vann Molyvann's Olympic Stadium (Khmer brutalist architecture from the 1960s), and the Independence Monument
Final stroll along Sisowath Quay - sunset over the Mekong, street food, and the feeling of a journey coming full circle

Essential Cambodia Circuit: Phnom Penh, Battambang and Angkor

Grand Tour of Cambodia: Mekong, Angkor, Battambang & Southern Coasts
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Arrival in Sen Monorom - capital of Mondulkiri perched at 800 meters altitude, nicknamed 'the Switzerland of Cambodia' for its rolling green hills, fresh air and peaceful atmosphere
Doh Kromom Mountain - two sacred hills for the Bunong people (Grandfather Hill and Grandmother Hill), panoramic viewpoint over the Mondulkiri highlands, perfect at sunset

Elephant Valley Project - ethical sanctuary run by an NGO, full day observing semi-wild elephants in the forest (no riding), you accompany Bunong mahouts and walk through the jungle alongside the pachyderms
Bousra Waterfall - the most impressive in Mondulkiri, 35 km from Sen Monorom, double cascade plunging 30 meters into the jungle, natural swimming pool at the base of the falls
Dak Dam Waterfall - on the same road as Bousra, setting of red earth and verdant hills, natural pools for picnicking and swimming

activities.text: Stroll through Sen Monorom - main street lined with guesthouses and restaurants, two lakes in the town center, local market where women from different ethnic minorities come to sell their produce
Encounter with a Bunong village - the Bunong (or Phnong) make up half the population of Mondulkiri, you discover their thatched-roof houses, traditional weaving and fermented rice wine
Visit to a Mondulkiri coffee plantation - coffee is organically grown by Phnong minorities using traditional methods supported by CEDAC, you follow the process from tree to cup and taste on site

days_plan.itin_title: Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines
Off the Beaten Track Cambodia: Mondulkiri, Ratanakiri & Wild Coastlines

visits.text: Orientation tour of Siem Reap - old French quarter, banks of the Siem Reap River, Psaar Chas (Old Market) where you'll find handicrafts, spices and souvenirs
Artisans d'Angkor - workshop-school founded in 1998 that trains young Cambodians in wood and stone carving, lacquer work and gilding; free workshop tours
Sunrise over Angkor Wat - arrive around 5 AM to watch the towers reflected in the pools; this 12th-century temple dedicated to Vishnu is the world's largest religious monument at 162 hectares

Angkor Thom and Bayon - fortified city spanning 9 km², you enter through the South Gate flanked by 108 statues - 54 gods and 54 demons holding a naga; the Bayon features 216 monumental faces carved in stone, dating from the reign of Jayavarman VII in the 13th century
Ta Prohm - 12th-century temple left in a semi-ruined state, giant roots of silk-cotton and strangler fig trees wrap around the galleries; made famous by the film Tomb Raider (2001)
Banteay Srei - nicknamed 'the citadel of women', this small 10th-century pink sandstone temple located 25 km from Angkor boasts the most finely carved bas-reliefs in all Khmer art
Preah Khan - vast monastic complex of 56 hectares built in 1191 by Jayavarman VII, labyrinthine galleries and a two-story building unique in Angkorian architecture
Terrace of Elephants and Terrace of the Leper King - royal platforms of Angkor Thom from which the king watched processions, 300-meter-long bas-reliefs depicting elephants, garudas and apsaras

Floating village of Kampong Phluk - houses on 8 to 10-meter stilts on Tonle Sap, Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake whose surface expands from 2,500 km² in dry season to 16,000 km² during monsoon

activities.text: Apsara dance show in the evening - recreation of sacred Khmer dances inscribed on UNESCO's intangible heritage list since 2003, accompanied by a buffet dinner at several restaurants in Siem Reap
Tuk-tuk rides through the Angkor Archaeological Park - Small Circuit route (17 km) connecting the main temples, your driver waits for you at each stop
Phare Ponleu Selpak circus show - troupe born in Battambang blending acrobatics, theater, live music and Cambodian social themes; performances every evening at 8 PM under a big top in Siem Reap
Boat ride through the flooded forest - rowing pirogue through the mangroves of Kampong Phluk, you glide beneath the submerged canopy during rainy season (October-March)

APOPO Visitor Center - demining center where African giant pouched rats are trained to detect landmines, 45-minute educational visit showing the decontamination work in Cambodia, one of the world's most heavily mined countries
